key_missing_perspectives: Temuan Orang Asli: only 46 acres customary land remain; pending native title claim filed 2023; demand FPIC
State planners/EXCO: land already excised earlier, current controls are EIA/planning, not retroactive 2011 law
Pro-road residents: traffic relief, cemetery as public facility, not purely extractive
B40 households: who captures road/development upside vs who loses free green space
Sabah/Sarawak: different forestry statutes; precedent overstated as uniform national
Youth activists: standing rule as generational injustice; climate standing for future generations
Women: Alicia Teoh as SACF founder; women as unpaid advocacy workers and everyday forest users
key_alternative_framings: Bureaucratic inertia, not conspiracy: 22-year delay may be delayed publication, not active concealment
KLNFR vs SACF difference may be legal (pre-gazette vs post-gazette), not just political calculus
Standing rule reflects legitimate finality principle; reform publication timelines instead
Fiscal federalism: states face incentives to monetise land; EFT mechanism exists but underfunded
Indigenous tenure: disputes recur because customary-land recognition is weak and FPIC sought too late
key_missing_facts: Section 11 public inquiry has NEVER been used in Selangor since 2011 enactment (importance: 0.9)
NFA Amendment Act 2022 only adopted in Perlis and Federal Territories as of March 2025 (importance: 0.9)
Temuan pending native title claim stayed pending FC ruling (importance: 0.9)
Three distinct forestry jurisdictions: Peninsular, Sabah, Sarawak (importance: 0.83)
Temuan customary land reduced to ~46 acres after previous development (importance: 0.86)
Road design includes box culvert as wildlife crossing (importance: 0.64)
Ecological Fiscal Transfers exist as structural solution but underutilised (importance: 0.72)
Section 12 replacement violated in at least 14 of 21 Bukit Cherakah excisions (importance: 0.8)
DASH EIA projected 30,000 vehicles/day on connecting roads; no cumulative impact assessment for SACF road (importance: 0.7)
international_parallels: India Forest Rights Act: community standing regardless of decision timing
Indonesia MK35: customary forests no longer state forests; tenure reset changes consultation requirements
South Africa NEMA: public interest standing for environmental challenges
Singapore: mandatory EIA for nature reserve development, no backdating
structural_solutions: Adopt NFA Amendment Act 2022 across all Peninsular states (SAM proposal)
Relax standing rules for environmental PIL (Malaysian Bar proposal)
Legally recognise Orang Asli customary land + require FPIC (CSO demand)
Strengthen Ecological Fiscal Transfers to make conservation fiscally viable for states
Rights-based environmental framework using constitutional quality-of-life jurisprudence
